ops.title: Serving decision 2026-07-19: build the hosted-9B lane in full; local 8B stays the serving default until a deliberate switch
ops.body:
User decision at the W4 decision point (evidence: cc-notes task f32fee09 — sweep ranking 9B 0.9371 > 8B 0.9301 > 4B 0.9262 > 35B-A3B 0.9230; Modal spike AUC parity 0.92953 hosted-bf16 vs 0.93169 local-4bit with hosted tracking the Tinker training substrate at median |Δ| 0.0022 vs local's 0.0251; provider matrix Modal-primary; latency budget fine_as_is).

**The decision**: build BOTH lanes to completion behind ONE serving abstraction in athome's serve layer — (1) the hosted lane: vLLM bf16 on Modal (pinned base revision + unfused LoRA, memory snapshots, scale-to-zero, explicit HTTP timeout in the drafter path), threshold calibrated through the endpoint per eval-what-you-serve, then a fresh 9B retrain promoted through the same hosted gate; and (3) the local lane: the lazy 4-bit MLX 8B daemon as-is. **Default = local (3) for now** — the hosted backend ships dark and the switch to it is a deliberate, explicit operator action, not a side effect of the build landing.

**Why**: hosted bf16 serves the model training actually produced (no 4-bit mid-confidence drift) and unlocks the 9B (+0.7pt, no mlx-lm LoRA path), but local is free, private, and proven; building both behind one contract lets the switch be a config flip judged on accrued evidence rather than a bet.

**Binding constraints for the build**: rapid-mlx-local and hosted are backends of the same serve contract (never parallel subsystems); fire thresholds are substrate-specific — each backend's promotion calibrates on its own served probs; the stage-1 lexical gate re-fit (P3 task 40ce24c) is substrate-independent and proceeds regardless; the stage-2 drafter threshold re-fit waits for whichever backend is active. Privacy: switching to hosted sends transcript windows off-machine — resurface at switch time.
